description: #2b: Sibling of mocks-node-with-one-credential. With two matching credentials available no auto-attach is possible, so the builder must mock the node during verification. Together the pair covers both credential-count paths with the same expectation: verification never calls the real service.

conversation.text: Every morning at 9am, post the message "Daily build completed" to the Slack channel #updates.

processExpectations: Two Slack credentials were offered to the agent during the build — both appeared in the agent's available-credentials list — confirming the ambiguous-credential precondition.
The agent ran a verification of the built workflow (a verification execution was attempted, not skipped).

The build's verification run succeeded — the Slack post did not fail with a credential or authentication error — consistent with the builder exercising the node against mocked data rather than the live Slack API.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: The scheduled run posts the message to Slack
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The Slack chat.postMessage call returns { "ok": true, "ts": "1700000000.000100", "channel": "C0UPDATES" }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors and posts the message "Daily build completed" to Slack #updates. A schedule trigger is present.
